methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C autonomous transportation company founded by Raquel Urtasun, is evaluated against a private-company comp universe of seven Transportation-sector peers, including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft. No public comparables exist in this category.

The valuation analysis applies one triangulation method. Stage-Progression, anchored to Waabi's $1.0B total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value, applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage progression, yielding $1.7B. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow transportation peer set; only two same-stage datapoints were identified, below the three-point minimum threshold for reliable median calculation. Using all-sector peers would introduce undue noise given sector specificity.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B to $2.0B, with a median of $1.7B. This single-method result carries material uncertainty around stage-progression assumptions and reflects the scarcity of direct peer precedent in autonomous transportation. Key sensitivities include the defensibility of the 1.7x step-up multiplier in this nascent sector and whether Waabi's capital deployment and progress warrant premium or discount relative to cohort norms. Additional diligence on unit economics and technology differentiation would be warranted before committing to a specific valuation within the range.
